test_first_gate.command: python3 -m unittest selected iteration-2 regression tests -v
test_first_gate.evidence: All 18 generic-scratch matrix rows, controlling-TTY group identity, launch-window no-release, validation cleanup, focused cleanup attribution, inherited-hook scrub, capability reporting, and create-issue unsafe-root regressions failed for their intended pre-fix reasons.
9488 passed, 0 failed, 1 skipped; only the dirty-tree stale-prose self-scan skipped. Focused Python suites passed 91 tests and the 18-row matrix plus resistant-process paths passed.
Five independent mutations proved scratch containment, controlling-TTY PGID preservation, cleanup-failure attribution, create-issue root validation, and host-capability reason reporting.

sweep_evidence.details: Grep covered every changed helper, hook, scratch owner/consumer, capability consumer, and both focused/full-suite boundaries. The mandatory comment/mechanism re-sweep independently found no stale or misleading prose. Ruff, bash -n, git diff --check, and touched-shell shellcheck were clean; full run.sh shellcheck was clean with its pre-existing parser codes excluded.

phase3_findings.description: Forced escalation could leave scratch owned by generic selectable modules outside the boundary root.
An unsupported-host matrix skip was invisible in the suite tally.
The launch-window hook remained blocked after a pending signal without an external release.
Validation rejection cleared the scratch handle before safe leaf cleanup.
Focused scratch cleanup failure was falsely reported as module process exit status 1.
Nested monitor mode could launch the worker in a different PGID under a controlling TTY.
SignalRowState does not encode boundary-specific path roles as a discriminated union.
The test-only PID reader accepts zero.

fix_decisions.evidence: Both boundaries export TMPDIR beneath the validated owned root; all 18 rows prove generic module scratch existed and was removed.
run.sh queries the capability helper and emits a host-capability suite skip on unsupported hosts.
The hook releases itself when either boundary records a pending signal; launch-window rows pass without creating .release.
Both boundaries perform only an empty-leaf rmdir for newly allocated but unvalidated scratch.
MODULE_CLEANUP_FAILED is tallied and recapped independently without overwriting MODULE_RC.
The supervisor disables nested monitor mode before worker launch and restores the caller mode afterward; a PTY regression proves identical PGIDs.

fix_decisions.claim_text: SignalRowState does not encode boundary-specific path roles as a discriminated union.
fix_decisions.evidence: parked-origin: below-threshold; the dataclass is now keyword-only, preventing positional Path-role swaps, while a boundary-specific union would be test-only structural refinement beyond the Important fix threshold.

fix_decisions.claim_text: The test-only PID reader accepts zero.
fix_decisions.evidence: parked-origin: below-threshold; the reader is test-evidence-only and kill -0 is a liveness probe rather than signal delivery. Retained for the final parked-class sweep.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Generic selectable-module scratch can escape boundary cleanup.
fix_decisions.evidence: The reproduced residue directly violates the issue's no-surviving-scratch contract after a bounded interruption.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Unsupported-host coverage loss is invisible in the suite tally.
fix_decisions.evidence: The prior path exited zero while silently providing no signal-matrix coverage, materially weakening a required test boundary.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Cleanup failure is reported as a module process exit.
fix_decisions.evidence: The diagnostic directly contradicted the observed successful module exit; self-contradicting changed behavior is non-demotable.

fix_decisions.claim_text: The worker can launch outside the supervisor PGID under a controlling TTY.
fix_decisions.evidence: A PTY reproduction observed split PGIDs, allowing forwarded signals and bounded escalation to miss the worker.
Boundary validation rejection safely discards empty leaves; create-issue self roots are physically parent-validated before recursive cleanup.
The generic runner fixture scrubs every signal-test hook; resistant parent and module delivery paths are covered.
The changeset states the POSIX signal/process-group scope and explicit unsupported-host skip.

convergence_inputs.reason: Four threshold fixes and a 358-line fix delta require a fresh review iteration.

verification.full_suite: 9488 passed, 0 failed, 1 expected dirty-tree stale-prose skip
verification.matrix_and_resistant_paths: 18 matrix rows and 3 resistant paths passed in 4.328s
verification.static: ruff, bash -n, diff-check, and touched-shell shellcheck clean

skipped_checks.reason: The suite ran before the fix commit while the working tree was dirty; the identical committed-HEAD stale-prose helper passed immediately after commit.

fix_delta_gate.evidence: The blinded reviewer was stopped before returning when the user requested an immediate stop at the already committed and pushed safe checkpoint.

devflow_reflection: iteration-2 fix committed and pushed after an UP_TO_DATE base checkpoint
fix-delta not verified: the in-flight blinded reviewer was stopped at the user's request
